title: hieracium froelichianum subsp. pseudopraealpinum
intro: Hieracium froelichianum subsp. pseudopraealpinum, also known as the Hieracium pseudopraealpinum or Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Hieracium froelichianum subsp. pseudopraealpinum have been reported to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
